title: Thalia lutea
intro: Thalia lutea, also known as Yellow Thalia, is a perennial aquatic plant native to the Americas, belonging to the family Marantaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thalia lutea include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Thalia lutea is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions.
